task_completion.description: Create the 'UserSessions' class with 'Token', 'Created', and 'UserEmail' properties
task_completion.visual_evidence: OrientDB Studio Schema tab showing the 'UserSessions' class with properties: 'Token' (String), 'Created' (DateTime), and 'UserEmail' (String).

task_completion.description: Populate the class with 5 active and 5 expired sessions
task_completion.visual_evidence: OrientDB Studio Browse/Query tab showing a query result of 'UserSessions' with 10 records: 5 with recent 'Created' timestamps (within the last hour) and 5 with older timestamps (approx 48 hours ago), containing real-looking emails.

task_completion.description: Create a server-side function named 'cleanup_sessions' to delete records older than 24 hours
task_completion.visual_evidence: OrientDB Studio Functions tab showing a function named 'cleanup_sessions' with code containing a DELETE statement targeting 'UserSessions' where the 'Created' date is older than 24 hours (e.g., using sysdate() - 86400000 or similar logic).

task_completion.description: Configure the OrientDB Scheduler to run the cleanup function every hour
task_completion.visual_evidence: OrientDB Studio OScheduler configuration or query showing a scheduled task linked to the 'cleanup_sessions' function with a rule/cron expression set to run every hour (e.g., '0 0 * * * ?' or interval of 3600000 ms).

task_completion.description: Execute the function and verify expired records are removed while active ones remain
task_completion.visual_evidence: OrientDB Studio Browse/Query tab showing the final state of the 'UserSessions' class with exactly 5 records remaining, all having recent 'Created' timestamps (the expired records should be gone).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OrientDB Studio GUI or a genuine database client
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OrientDB web interface (Studio) or a database terminal client being used, rather than just a text editor with scripts.

integrity.description: The cleanup function contains actual deletion logic
integrity.visual_evidence: The function body in the screenshots shows a real SQL DELETE command or equivalent JS API call, not just a mock or empty function.

integrity.description: Test data uses real emails from the Profiles class
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails visible in the UserSessions records look like genuine seeded data (e.g., realistic names/domains) rather than placeholder strings like 'test1@test.com'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Database: demodb. Class: UserSessions with properties Token (String), Created (DateTime), UserEmail (String). Function: cleanup_sessions containing DELETE logic for records older than 24 hours (often calculated as 86400000 milliseconds). Scheduler must be configured to run this function hourly. Final state should show 0 expired records and 5 active records remaining.
